vocab.meaning: for the sake of, on behalf of

grammar.title: ‘Por esta razón’
grammar.detail: ‘For this reason / therefore’ — fixed Pauline connector. Spanish uses ‘por’ + abstract noun without an article (‘por esta razón’, not ‘por la razón’).

grammar.title: Reflexive verb ‘me arrodillo’
grammar.detail: arrodillarse = to kneel oneself down. Always reflexive in Spanish: yo me arrodillo, tú te arrodillas, …

expression.note: Paul reframes his Roman imprisonment as service to Christ. His chains are Christ’s, not Caesar’s.

vocab.meaning: to find out, to hear about

grammar.title: Present perfect ‘se han enterado’
grammar.detail: Reflexive verb in present perfect: ‘you (formal pl.) have come to know’. The reflexive pronoun (se) sits before the auxiliary haber, not the participle.

grammar.title: ‘sin duda’
grammar.detail: An adverbial phrase meaning ‘surely / no doubt’. Often used at the start of a clause as a polite assertion: ‘Surely you’ve heard…’

expression.phrase: el plan de la gracia de Dios
expression.note: ‘the plan / administration of God’s grace’ — NVI smooths the Greek ‘oikonomia’ (stewardship) into a more accessible word.

vocab.meaning: mystery (in NT sense: God’s plan now revealed)

grammar.title: ‘es decir’
grammar.detail: ‘that is to say / namely’ — a connector used to clarify what was just said. Like English ‘i.e.’

grammar.title: Preterite ‘me dio a conocer’
grammar.detail: Preterite of dar (he gave) + a + infinitive conocer. The combination means ‘made known to me’ — completed action in the past.

expression.phrase: el misterio que me dio a conocer por revelación
expression.note: In Paul, ‘misterio’ is not something puzzling — it is something previously hidden and now openly revealed. The means of disclosure: ‘por revelación’ (through revelation), not investigation.
